title: Black Mountain in N.H. will not become a community co-op as planned
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Black Mountain in Jackson, N.H., will not become a community co-op as initially planned.
Erik Mogensen, GM and owner, plans to use Black Mountain to tackle challenges facing independent ski areas.
Mogensen is moving the headquarters of Indy Pass and Entabeni Systems to Jackson, N.H.

title: Why a 35-year-old breast cancer survivor is running the Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Julia Aldrich, 35, will run her fourth marathon at the 2026 Boston Marathon, her first time running for charity.
She was diagnosed with breast cancer at 32 and resumed running races months after her double mastectomy.
Aldrich aims to raise funds for the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, where she received care, by running the marathon.

title: An expert explains why the recent Danvers murder stands out
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, 18, allegedly murdered 68-year-old Janet Swallows in her Danvers home, with no apparent motive.
James Alan Fox explains teenagers, like DeMayo, may kill without logical reasons due to underdeveloped prefrontal cortex.
DeMayo pleads not guilty to murder and home invasion; held without bail for evaluation at Bridgewater State Hospital.

title: MGB researchers just developed a new risk calculator for obesity. Here’s why that’s a game changer.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mass General Brigham researchers developed a genetic risk calculator to predict obesity and Type 2 diabetes long-term effects.
The tool uses a polygenic risk score aggregating genetic variants and spans data from over 8.5 million global participants.
The score enhances predictive accuracy across multiple populations, focusing on African, East Asian, and South Asian ancestries.

title: Why this ICU nurse is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support trauma recovery
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Erin Daniel, a 25-year-old ICU nurse from Mansfield, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Daniel is part of the Stepping Strong team supporting Brigham and Women's Hospital's trauma recovery initiatives.
Inspired by Gillian Reny's story, Daniel aims to advance trauma research and patient rehabilitation through her marathon run.

title: Poll: Mass. Legislature underwater in court of public opinion
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts poll shows 51% view Legislature unfavorably, with only 28% favorable.
86% of poll respondents support subjecting lawmakers to state public records law.
83% of respondents want Legislature to permit State Auditor's audit despite current resistance.

title: Police seek Mass. vehicle after driver involved in fatal Providence hit-and-run
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Providence police search for a black Chevrolet Equinox with Massachusetts plates in a fatal hit-and-run case.
Kevin Chappie, 46, was killed after being struck while using a crosswalk on Corliss Street early Saturday.
Public urged to contact Providence Police Department with information at 401-243-6279 or 401-243-6285.

title: Martha Stewart’s The Bedford is now open at this New England resort
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: The Bedford by Martha Stewart has opened at Foxwoods Resort Casino in Connecticut.
The restaurant serves Stewart's favorite recipes, including pierogies and honey-mustard salmon.
The Bedford spans 10,000 square feet and can seat up to 204 guests.

title: Weekend commuter rail disruption will now leak into Fridays
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MBTA and Keolis are extending weekend commuter rail shutdowns into Fridays at North Station for signal upgrades.
Commuter rail service will be impacted on March 20-22, March 27-29, April 10-12, and April 24-27.
Shuttle and Orange Line services will replace trains on affected lines, causing potential travel delays up to 70 minutes.

title: This Massachusetts commute ranks as the most stressful, poll finds
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: The Zakim Bridge/I-93 North in Boston is ranked as the most stressful commute in Massachusetts.
The Southeast Expressway and I-95/Route 128 in Waltham follow as the second and third most stressful commutes.
I-95 is noted as a significant stress point throughout New England, affecting several states beyond Massachusetts.
